annotation_target.structure: Developmental resolution — triplets settle, E major returns
teaching_goals: feel the release as harmony returns home

teaching_notes.note: Full resolution: the triplets settle, the key is clear again.
technique: let the phrase breathe at the resolution

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 13–16 of Arabesque No. 1 in E Major (continuation of mm. 9–12), analyze the musical continuation, play in a loop.

session.content: Play measures 13–16 and describe how the harmony resolves.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 13–16.
Measures 13–16 resolve the developmental tension back toward E major stability. The triplet texture softens and the phrase settles. A gentle return to the opening character. Let me loop mm. 13–16.
Debussy Arabesque No. 1 mm. 13–16: harmonic resolution, settling back to E major character. Pairs with mm. 9–12.
